duplicate_rule_sources.notes: ARCHITECTURE.md mirrors RULES.md with no new information; acceptable for reference clarity

duplicate_rule_sources.content: Card schema and CardID generation template
duplicate_rule_sources.notes: Schema is authoritative runtime; RULES.md documents the intent; no conflict

engine_logic_without_rule_reference.rule_mapping: Partially documented in RULES.md §4 (Turn Lifecycle); full FSM transitions require reference to engine code
Not in rules scope (RULES.md §1.2 Explicit Non-Scope: 'Bot AI logic')

event_docs: docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md — Transaction Log Entry Structure, Data Flow
event_model_verification.event_schema_explanation: Documented: event id, parentId, type, name, timestamp, payload, status; hierarchy (Trace/Match → Span/Turn → Child Span/Phase → Event)
event_model_verification.replay_mechanics_detail: Documented in RULES.md §18 and ARCHITECTURE.md: stateHashBefore/After per action, canonical serialization, identical specVersion/params/preState/turnInput MUST produce identical postState/stateHashAfter
missing_event_topics: Event envelope wire format (JSON schema not explicitly documented outside of code)
Exact event ID generation algorithm (referenced as 'Unique identifier' but generation method not specified in docs)

architecture_documents: docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md — System Overview, Packages, Turn Lifecycle, Event Sourcing & Determinism, Data Flow
missing_topics: Error recovery and failure modes (partially in RISKS.md, not in architecture)
Backward compatibility policy for schema/event changes
Security architecture (auth model, anti-cheat, cheating vectors)
Observability architecture (OTLP described in README, architecture docs reference it but don't detail it)
architecture_docs_check.architecture_quality: solid for single-match deterministic flow; gaps in deployment, scaling, and failure recovery architecture

architecture_vs_runtime_drift.drift: Architecture describes 7 phases abstractly; actual runtime state machine includes micro-step transitions (system:advance, system:victory) not mentioned in architecture doc
architecture_vs_runtime_drift.resolution: Documented in DECISIONS.md (Unit 2A); intentional: ARCHITECTURE.md is descriptive, STATE_MACHINE is authoritative runtime

architecture_vs_runtime_drift.drift: Documentation mentions 'phaseTrace' and 'phaseTraceDigest' as optional fields; schema confirms structure; no conflict detected

inconsistent_usage.issue: Term used in future work; no canonical game-mode specification yet (deferred)

gameplay_docs_check.ui_documentation: Not present (client UI is self-documenting in code)

documentation_strengths: ✓ Canonical rules specification (docs/RULES.md v1.0) is comprehensive, precise, and deterministic — ideal for a rules engine
✓ High rule-to-code traceability: every major rule section maps to engine components (engine/src/combat.ts, engine/src/turns.ts, engine/src/state.ts)
✓ Authority model explicitly defined in DECISIONS.md (Unit 2A): RULES.md = normative, shared schemas = contracts, engine FSM = runtime, ARCHITECTURE = descriptive
✓ Event model documented with hierarchical structure (Trace/Match → Span/Turn → Phase → Event); Structured Event Model in RULES.md §17 is precise
✓ Deterministic replay guarantees documented (RULES.md §18) with hash algorithm and canonical serialization requirements
✓ Hard invariants explicitly listed (RULES.md §19); no silent assumptions
✓ Developer setup is clear and quick (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md provide working local dev path in <5 minutes)
✓ Operational hazards captured (RISKS.md covers 5 critical DEV gotchas with mitigation)
✓ Multi-session resumable work plan (FUTURE.md tracks ongoing fidelity units with explicit progress checkpoints)
✓ Terminology is mostly consistent; canonical vocabulary in RULES.md §2 serves as reference
✓ All major packages (shared, engine, server, client) are documented in ARCHITECTURE.md with clear boundaries
critical_gaps: ✗ Deployment & scaling architecture not documented — single-server assumed, no multi-instance strategy mentioned
✗ Security architecture missing — no threat model, auth strategy, anti-cheat vectors documented
✗ Error recovery and failure modes largely absent from architecture (covered only in RISKS.md as hazards, not in system design)
✗ Network protocol versioning strategy undefined — schema evolution and backwards compatibility policy not documented
✗ Performance & scalability targets not stated (throughput, latency, concurrent match limits)
✗ Debugging guide missing — developers must infer debug techniques from code or RISKS.md
✗ New rule / schema change process not documented — no contribution runbook
✗ Backwards compatibility / migration strategy for rules/schema changes not defined
✗ Arena and Siege format specifications are directory stubs (docs/formats/arena/, docs/formats/siege/) — no actual format documentation
✗ Player-facing quick-start / tutorial missing (rules docs are authoritative but dense for onboarding)

recommended_improvements.improvement: Document threat model, auth strategy (current: guest alias + future account model), anti-cheat mechanisms (replay integrity as primary control). Reference docs/system/DECISIONS.md mode policy matrix (Casual, Ranked-like, Official) as foundation.
recommended_improvements.impact: Blocks ranked/official deployment; security assumptions are implicit

recommended_improvements.improvement: Document deployment strategy: single-server (current), future clustering/replication, state persistence backend choice (in-memory now, long-term store TBD). Link to docs/system/FUTURE.md Unit 2D (public event stream, analytics persistence).
recommended_improvements.impact: Production readiness; infrastructure decisions not yet captured

recommended_improvements.improvement: Define versioning policy: specVersion in match params (current: '1.0'); event schema versioning; wire protocol versioning. Document breaking change policy and migration strategy.
recommended_improvements.impact: Future-proofs against schema drift; enables long-term API stability

recommended_improvements.improvement: Add docs/CONTRIBUTING_ADVANCED.md or section in CONTRIBUTING.md covering: adding new rules (process, docs/code/tests sync), schema changes (impact on all packages), state machine modifications, determinism testing.
recommended_improvements.impact: Lowers barrier for external contributors; reduces questions/PRs needing rework

recommended_improvements.improvement: Document system-level failure handling: network disconnects, state divergence, unrecoverable errors (already partially in RULES.md §17.3). Expand RISKS.md to include production failure scenarios.
recommended_improvements.impact: Operators gain confidence in fault tolerance; improves debugging in prod

recommended_improvements.improvement: Document testing pyramid (unit/integration/e2e), test execution commands, coverage targets, replay verification testing. Reference existing engine/tests/state-machine.test.ts as example.
recommended_improvements.impact: Standardizes test expectations; guides PR reviewers

recommended_improvements.improvement: Complete docs/formats/arena/RULES.md and docs/formats/siege/RULES.md with format-specific rules (board geometry, player counts, victory conditions, phase adjustments).
recommended_improvements.impact: Enables future format implementation; currently blocked by missing specs

recommended_improvements.improvement: Create docs/GAMEPLAY_QUICKSTART.md: plain-language rules summary, worked examples, common scenarios. Keep rules/RULES.md as authoritative spec.
recommended_improvements.impact: Improves player first-time experience; reduces support questions

recommended_improvements.improvement: Document performance targets (match creation latency, action resolution latency, concurrent match limits). Document OTLP metric/trace strategy and alerting thresholds.
recommended_improvements.impact: Enables SRE onboarding; supports load testing and optimization

recommended_improvements.improvement: Document JSON schema for public event envelopes (match_created, action_taken, turn_completed, match_ended). Link to docs/system/FUTURE.md Unit 2D (public event stream design).

documentation_drift_controls.drift_guard_location: docs/system/DECISIONS.md (explicit authority boundaries); CI check pnpm rules:check (phase-order + FSM drift)
known_guardrails: DECISIONS.md DEC-2A-002: 'Any runtime behavior change must update docs/RULES.md in the same unit or add a temporary divergence note'
DECISIONS.md DEC-2A-003: 'Drift guardrails are CI-enforced (pnpm rules:check) for phase-order docs consistency and stale FSM doc references'
CONTRIBUTING.md PR guidelines: 'Update docs when behavior, commands, or operator workflows change'
improvement_recommendations: Add a pre-commit hook or CI check that fails if RULES.md version is not bumped when engine logic changes (prevent silent drift)
Add a doc linter that checks for broken references to missing files (e.g., docs/system/GAME_STATE_MACHINE.md if not present)
Create a 'documentation checklist' template in PR template: '[x] RULES.md updated', '[x] ARCHITECTURE.md consistent', '[x] schema examples current'

criteria: ✓ Canonical rules specification exists and is version-controlled (v1.0)
✓ System architecture is documented with package boundaries and data flow
✓ Drift guards are in place (CI checks, decision register, PR guidelines)
✓ Developer setup is clear and tested

✗ Does not yet qualify as 'verifiable-specification' because: no public-facing verification profile docs (Unit 2C deferred), no offline third-party verification spec, event wire format not formally spec'd, Arena/Siege formats not documented

steps_required.title: Complete Unit 2C — Verification Profiles (standard / official)
steps_required.description: Define hash/signature boundaries, provenance binding, JWKS distribution, match-level policy immutability. Document 'standard' (core deterministic replay hash) vs 'official' (higher-fidelity provenance, third-party verifiable).
steps_required.documentation_impact: docs/RULES.md (add normative profile sections), docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md, shared/src/schema.ts

steps_required.title: Complete Unit 2D — Public Event Stream Envelope Design
steps_required.description: Define canonical internal event envelopes, derived audience streams (public spectator, audit, analytics), privacy/redaction policy, post-match hidden-state reveal timing (endOfMatch default, endOfRound option, 0 delay support).
steps_required.documentation_impact: docs/RULES.md (event envelope normative spec), shared/src/schema.ts (event types), docs/system/ARCHITECTURE.md

steps_required.title: Formalize Event Wire Format Specification
steps_required.description: Publish JSON Schema for all public event types (match_created, action_taken, turn_completed, match_ended, round_boundary). Include verification profile metadata hooks and signature fields.

steps_required.title: Document Arena & Siege Format Specifications
steps_required.description: Complete docs/formats/arena/RULES.md and docs/formats/siege/RULES.md with format-specific rules. Each must define board geometry, player count, victory conditions, phase adjustments relative to Duel.

steps_required.title: Add Executable Documentation Layer (Optional but Recommended)
steps_required.description: Implement rules scenarios as testable fixtures. Generate state-machine diagrams from code. Add documentation verification tests that check for broken references, stale examples, schema drift.

steps_required.title: Publish Third-Party Verification Guide
steps_required.description: Document how an external party can verify an 'official' match offline: public key fetch, signature validation, replay hash computation, determinism verification. Include concrete worked examples.

key_findings: Phalanx Duel has solid, verifiable-specification-adjacent documentation. RULES.md is authoritative and comprehensive; rule-to-code traceability is high (92%).
Authority model is explicit (DECISIONS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, ARCHITECTURE.md); drift guards are CI-enforced.
Critical gaps are primarily in security architecture, deployment strategy, schema evolution, and format specifications (Arena/Siege).
Current documentation is sufficient for v1.0 Duel development, testing, and local onboarding.
To reach 'verifiable-specification' maturity, complete Units 2C (verification profiles), 2D (event stream), arena/siege format specs, and third-party verification guide.
summary.readiness_for_production: Ready for single-server, internal-play production. Gaps in security/deployment strategy and arena/siege specs block ranked/tournament/multi-format production.
summary.readiness_for_external_contribution: Good. Setup is clear; drift guards are in place. Missing advanced contribution guide (schema changes, new rules, determinism testing).
audit_recommendations_priority: HIGH: Schema Evolution Policy (enables long-term stability)
MEDIUM: Developer Contribution Guide (enables external contribution)
